era_context: The early 1780s mark the final stages of the American Revolutionary War and the beginning of a new era in world politics. The British Empire is facing the loss of its American colonies but is consolidating power elsewhere globally. The Enlightenment is at its height in Europe. China under the Qianlong Emperor is at the apex of Qing power. The Ottoman Empire continues its slow decline. The Industrial Revolution is accelerating in Britain.

events.title: Preliminary Treaty of Paris signed, ending American Revolutionary War
events.description: The preliminary Treaty of Paris is signed on 30 November 1782 (or 3 September 1783, depending on the source and calendar system), formally ending hostilities between Great Britain and the United States. The treaty recognizes American independence and establishes the Mississippi River as the western boundary of the new nation. This preliminary treaty is later superseded by the definitive Treaty of Paris of 1783 or 1785, depending on the source.

sources.name: Official treaty text and diplomatic correspondence
Diplomatic records of the United States and Great Britain

events.certainty_note: The treaty date is well-documented, though there is some confusion in historical sources about whether the preliminary or definitive treaty should be dated to 1782 or 1783-1785.

events.title: Battle of the Saintes
events.description: The Battle of the Saintes is fought on 9-12 April 1782 between the British Royal Navy and the French Navy in the waters off Dominica in the Caribbean. The British, under Admiral George Rodney, defeat the French fleet, capturing several ships and killing the French admiral, the Comte de Grasse. The victory is a significant boost to British morale during the American Revolutionary War and demonstrates British naval superiority, though it does not fundamentally alter the course of the war.

sources.name: British Admiralty records and official reports
Contemporary accounts by naval officers and observers

events.certainty_note: The battle date and details are well-documented in naval records and contemporary accounts.

events.title: Rama I (Chakri) defeats Burmese forces at Chiang Mai
events.description: General Chakri (later Rama I), who is consolidating power in Siam, defeats Burmese forces at Chiang Mai. The victory strengthens Chakri's position and demonstrates his military prowess. These military successes contribute to Chakri's eventual assumption of power in 1783 and his coronation as Rama I in 1784.

sources.name: Thai royal chronicles and court records

events.certainty_note: The battle is attested in Thai and Burmese chronicles, though the exact date and details may vary depending on the source.

events.title: Qianlong Emperor appoints Heshen as Grand Councillor
events.description: The Qianlong Emperor appoints Heshen, a young Manchu official, as Grand Councillor and grants him unprecedented power and influence. Heshen becomes one of the most powerful officials in the Qing government and a close confidant of the aging emperor. Heshen's rise to power marks the beginning of a period of increasing corruption and factional conflict within the Qing bureaucracy, contributing to the empire's gradual decline.

sources.name: Qing imperial records and court diaries

events.certainty_note: Heshen's appointment is documented in imperial records, though the exact date may vary slightly depending on the source.

events.title: Tipu Sultan becomes de facto ruler of Mysore
events.description: Tipu Sultan, the son of Hyder Ali, becomes the de facto ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore, though his father remains nominally in power until his death in 1783. Tipu Sultan begins implementing military reforms and modernizing the kingdom's administration. His rise to power marks the beginning of a period of significant military innovation and cultural patronage in South India.

sources.name: Mysore court records and administrative documents

events.certainty_note: Tipu Sultan's rise to de facto power is well-documented, though the exact date of his assumption of full authority is somewhat ambiguous.

events.title: Rama III (future king) born in Siam
events.description: Prince Rama III is born in Siam. He is the son of Chakri (later Rama I) and will become the third king of the Chakri Dynasty, ruling from 1824 to 1851. His birth represents the continuation of the royal line that will rule Thailand into the modern era.

sources.name: Thai royal chronicles and court records

events.certainty_note: The birth date is recorded in Thai royal chronicles, though the exact date may vary depending on the calendar system used.

events.title: James Watt patents the steam engine with separate condenser
events.description: James Watt receives a patent for his improved steam engine with a separate condenser, a design that significantly increases the efficiency of steam engines. The patent represents a major innovation in the development of steam power and becomes crucial to the Industrial Revolution. Watt's improvements make steam engines practical for a wide range of industrial applications, from textile mills to mines.
events.certainty_note: The patent date and details are well-documented in official records.

events.title: Rousseau's 'Confessions' published posthumously
events.description: Jean-Jacques Rousseau's 'Confessions,' a groundbreaking autobiographical work, is published posthumously in 1782. The work, written in the 1760s, presents Rousseau's life and thoughts in an intimate and often controversial manner. The 'Confessions' becomes an influential text in the development of Romanticism and modern autobiography, emphasizing individual experience and emotion over abstract reason.
events.certainty_note: Publication date and details are well-documented.

disconfirming_evidence: The Treaty of Paris (1782) is sometimes presented as definitively ending the American Revolutionary War, but the treaty was preliminary and was superseded by later treaties. The exact date of the treaty varies in sources depending on whether the preliminary or definitive version is being referenced and which calendar system is used. Additionally, the Battle of the Saintes, while a significant British naval victory, is sometimes overstated in its strategic importance; it did not fundamentally alter the course of the American Revolutionary War. Heshen's appointment as Grand Councillor is sometimes portrayed as the beginning of Qing decline, but modern scholarship emphasizes that the empire remained powerful and prosperous for several more decades.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 1782 is rich for European and North American events, particularly those involving elite political and military figures. Scientific and technological innovations are well-recorded. However, the record is sparse for non-elite populations, women, and non-Western regions outside of major political events. Southeast Asian and South Asian sources are available but often filtered through later compilations or colonial-era interpretations. The historical record reflects the biases of literate elites and colonial powers; the experiences of enslaved people, peasants, and indigenous populations are largely absent from contemporary documentation. The publication of Rousseau's 'Confessions' is well-documented, but the work's influence on subsequent thought is sometimes overstated in popular accounts.

graph_edges.note: Preliminary Treaty of Paris (1782) led to the definitive Treaty of Paris (1783)
Battle of the Saintes occurred during the final stages of the American Revolutionary War, which ended with the Treaty of Paris in 1783
Chakri's military victories contributed to his assumption of power in 1783
Heshen's rise to power contributed to factional conflicts that affected Qing governance until his downfall after the Qianlong Emperor's death
Tipu Sultan's rise to de facto power led to his formal assumption of power after Hyder Ali's death in 1783
Watt's steam engine patent led to the establishment of the Boulton & Watt partnership in 1784
